topic: Claude Code Task Budgets Guide 2026: Control Token Spend in Agentic Sessions

key_points: Official Anthropic docs: task budgets are in public beta on Claude Opus 4.7 with 'task-budgets-2026-03-13' beta header
Add task_budget to output_config with type 'tokens' and total token count
Budget is advisory (soft hint), not a hard cap — max_tokens remains the enforced ceiling
Minimum accepted task_budget.total is 20,000 tokens
The remaining field allows carrying budget across context compaction
Works with effort parameter: effort = depth per step, task budget = total breadth
Adaptive thinking naturally scales down as budget depletes
Only supported on Claude Opus 4.7 at launch
Average enterprise cost is ~$13/developer/active day and $150-250/month
90% of users stay below $30 per active day
Agent teams use ~7x more tokens than standard sessions in plan mode
Claude Code auto-compaction achieves 60-80% context size reduction
Rate limit recommendations: 200k-300k TPM per user for teams of 1-5
Extended thinking (xhigh/max effort) costs tens of thousands of tokens per request
Background processes consume under $0.04 per session in tokens
Three core mechanisms: hard context limits, automatic compaction, pre-execution budget checks
Multi-agent pipelines exponentially accumulate context: 3-agent system can balloon orchestrator by 60,000+ tokens across 10 cycles
Tiered warnings at 70%, 85%, and 90% of limits give progressive alerts
Testing vs production disparity: production sessions accumulate history causing unexpected cost explosions
Compaction is lossy compression — some details may be discarded
Task budgets transform agentic sessions from a 'mistake factory' to a 'review queue'
/config task_budget 50000 sets budget in Claude Code CLI
When approaching limit, Claude pauses and reports: '28/35 done, est 8k to finish'
xhigh effort is now the default in Claude Code; increases token spend 1-1.35x over Opus 4.6
Recommend high effort for deterministic tasks, xhigh for judgment-heavy work
Three major token drains: tool call outputs, context accumulation, system overhead
Real case: $1,600 invoice when Claude Code usage ramped up
40-70% savings reported from optimization techniques
Keep CLAUDE.md under 500 tokens with only essential rules
Create .claudeignore to exclude build dirs and logs
Use /compact to summarize long conversations rather than carrying full context
Token allocation by plan: Pro ~44k tokens/5h, Max5 ~88k, Max20 ~220k
Opus 4.7 costs ~1.7x more than Sonnet 4.6
April 2026 tokenizer update can inflate costs up to 35% depending on content type
Peak-hour burn (weekday 5-11am Pacific) runs 1.3-1.5x faster
Engineering leaders must track both leading (PR review time) and lagging (incident rate) indicators

stats.fact: Average enterprise Claude Code cost is ~$13 per developer per active day and $150-250 per developer per month
90% of enterprise Claude Code users stay below $30 per active day
Agent teams use approximately 7x more tokens than standard sessions when teammates run in plan mode
Single agentic prompts can burn 50,000-300,000 tokens; users report single prompts eating 30-90% of a 5-hour budget
Claude Code auto-compaction achieves 60-80% context size reduction
A 3-agent pipeline can balloon orchestrator context by 60,000+ tokens across just 10 cycles
Opus 4.7 costs approximately 1.7x more than Sonnet 4.6
April 2026 tokenizer update inflates effective costs up to 35%, with code hitting the upper range
The minimum accepted task_budget.total is 20,000 tokens; values below return a 400 error
xhigh effort (default in Claude Code) increases token spend 1-1.35x compared to Opus 4.6
Token allocation by plan: Pro ~44,000 tokens per 5-hour window, Max5 ~88,000, Max20 ~220,000

angles: Task budgets vs max_tokens: they are complementary, not competing — budget is advisory breadth control, max_tokens is the hard per-request ceiling
The 'countdown' mechanism: how Claude sees a server-side budget marker that updates as it generates thinking, tool calls, and output
The 'graceful finish' advantage: Claude pauses and reports progress when budget runs low, instead of silent cutoff
Carrying budget across context compaction with the 'remaining' field — critical for long multi-turn agentic loops
The too-small budget trap: budgets that are clearly insufficient cause refusal-like behavior or aggressive scope reduction
Measuring before setting: run tasks without task_budget first to find your p99 spend distribution, then tune
Task budget + effort interaction: effort controls reasoning depth per step, task budget controls total work across the loop
Multi-agent cost multiplication: 7x token multiplier in plan mode means task budgets are critical for agent team deployments
Practical CLI vs API: /config task_budget in Claude Code CLI vs output_config.task_budget in the Messages API
keywords: how to limit Claude Code token spend
